metadata.title: Build an Uncertainty Squeeze Sim with Claude Code
metadata.color_semantics: TEAL = hyperbola boundary and original state. CRIMSON = squeezed state. INK for all Text.

beats.narration_text: Medhavy A-I. From Sanskrit — meaning intelligent, or intellectually brilliant. The perfect name for our A-I-powered intelligent learning systems.

The prompt. Claude Code terminal: claude — plot the Heisenberg uncertainty boundary as a hyperbola in sigma-x sigma-p space. Mark two points on the curve: sigma-x equals one nanometer, and sigma-x equals zero-point-five nanometers. Show that halving sigma-x doubles sigma-p. The product is always h-bar over two — not h-bar, not h-bar over four. Specify the formula and the two state points.

props.command:
claude "plot uncertainty boundary:
  σ_x σ_p = ℏ/2  (minimum-uncertainty hyperbola)
  mark state A: σ_x = 1 nm  → σ_p = ℏ/(2×1nm)
  mark state B: σ_x = 0.5nm → σ_p = ℏ/(2×0.5nm)
  show doubling"

beats.narration_text: The CLI writes it. Read before running. Find the line for sigma-p. It should read: h-bar divided by open-paren two times sigma-x close-paren. If it reads h-bar divided by sigma-x — no factor of two — the boundary lands at the wrong constant. The Kennard inequality uses h-bar over two. The Heisenberg rough estimate uses h-bar. They are not the same. Thirty seconds. Catch it before the render.

props.code:
# squeeze.py  —  Claude Code output
import numpy as np
hbar = 1.0546e-34   # J·s

def sigma_p(sx_nm):             # ← the formula
    sx = sx_nm * 1e-9
    return hbar / (2 * sx)     # ← must be 2*sx

# sigma_x=1nm  → sigma_p=5.27e-26 kg·m/s
# sigma_x=0.5nm→ sigma_p=1.054e-25 kg·m/s
# ratio = 2.000  ✓

beats.narration_text: Run it. Two checks. First: both state points fall exactly on the teal hyperbola — they are minimum-uncertainty Gaussian states. They sit on the boundary, not above it. Second: the sigma-p for the squeezed state is exactly double the sigma-p for the unsqueezed state. The ratio reads two-point-zero-zero-zero. Push sigma-x down; sigma-p must go up, by exactly the same factor.

Iterate: claude — update squeeze dot py — shade the region above the hyperbola in light teal, label it allowed. The boundary is the minimum. Everything above it is allowed. Everything below it is forbidden — no quantum state can live there. One diff, one re-run. Prompt, read, run, check, change. The uncertainty principle was the vehicle. The loop is the takeaway.

props.command:
claude "update squeeze.py:
  → shade region σ_x σ_p > ℏ/2 (allowed)
  → label 'allowed' and 'forbidden'"

beats.narration_text: Nik Bear Brown. Brutalist and Educational A-I. On YouTube, at Nik Bear Brown. Nik Bear Brown dot com.
